channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas reports no progress in talks with Israel on ceasefire's second phase
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The latest round of talks on the second phase of the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas has made “no progress,” and it's unclear whether they will resume on Saturday, a senior Hamas official said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrians begin fasting during first Ramadan without Assad family rule in decades
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Some restaurants and coffee shops in Syria were closed during the day Saturday while others opened as usual as observant Muslims began fasting during the holy month of Ramadan, the first since the fall of Assad family rule in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's military is told to prepare to defend a Druze community outside Syria's capital
item.description: Israel’s defense ministry says the military has been instructed to prepare to defend a Druze settlement in the suburbs of Damascus in neighboring Syria.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy embraced by British prime minister a day after White House blowout
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer embraced Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Saturday and told him he had the nation's unwavering support a day after the blowout at the White House with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vatican gives health update as Pope enters third week in hospital
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is stable, and he is alternating between mechanical ventilation and high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey's Kurds wary of path to peace after PKK declares ceasefire
item.description: DIYARBAKIR, Turkey (AP) — A momentous ceasefire declaration on Saturday stirred a mix of emotions in southeast Turkey and northern Iraq, where people bore the brunt of the 40-year conflict between Kurdish militants and the Turkish state.
item.dc:creator: MUCAHIT CEYLAN and ADNAN AHMAD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Namibian founding president Sam Nujoma is laid to rest and praised as the last African liberator
item.description: Sam Nujoma, Namibia’s founding president who steered the country to freedom from apartheid South Africa, has been laid to rest in the country’s Heroes Acre cemetery following a state funeral attended by African leaders and foreign dignitaries.
item.dc:creator: Sonja Smith, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Countries around the world already are feeling the impact of the Trump administration’s decision to eliminate more than 90% of foreign aid contracts and cut some $60 billion in funding.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Wilson Mcmakin And Monika Pronczuk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palestinian businessman lobbies DC with long-shot Gaza vision
item.description: A Palestinian businessman is making a long-shot bid to lead a new governing body of the Gaza Strip, putting up an estimated $300,000 for lobbying services to influence Washington and the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis had peaceful night, is resting after breathing crisis, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, had a peaceful night and is resting after a breathing crisis, the Vatican said Saturday. He has been in the hospital since Feb. 14.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sean Penn: Zelenskyy-Trump fight shouldn't eclipse Russia's crimes
item.description: “Remember where we really stand and what this is really about,” Sean Penn implored on Friday's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainians rally around Zelenskyy as defender of national interest after Oval Office blowout
item.description: Ukrainians have rallied around President Volodymyr Zelenskyy as a defender of their country’s interests in the hours after he left the White House after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rose Girone, oldest living Holocaust survivor, dies at 113
item.description: Rose Girone, the oldest living survivor of the Holocaust and advocate for sharing the stories of survivors, died on Monday in New York at age 113.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Getting the Israel-Hamas ceasefire to the next phase will be difficult. Here's why
item.description: Israel and Hamas have begun working to advance their ceasefire agreement in Gaza to the next phase, but it's unclear if they'll get there and, if not, what comes next.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's the real reason Trump and Zelenskyy's deal blew up in the Oval Office
item.description: A meeting between Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and President Donald Trump exploded after Zelenskyy rejected a deal that did not include security guarantees for his country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lebanon seizes $2.5M destined for Hezbollah after stopping man at airport in 'unprecedented’ bust
item.description: A man allegedly carrying $2.5 million in cash destined for Hezbollah reportedly was detained at Beirut’s airport in what an expert is calling an "unprecedented" bust.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis' condition improving but will remain 'guarded' until he is stable for several days: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is improving after spending the past two weeks hospitalized with double pneumonia, but the Vatican said his prognosis is "guarded" until he's stable for a few more days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A good rapport but mixed signals on Ukraine: Takeaways from Keir Starmer's trip to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prime Minister Keir Starmer wore a broad smile aboard the plane home from Washington. He landed back in Britain on Friday with the satisfaction of a tricky mission not quite accomplished, but off to a flying start.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin 'doesn't want peace,' EU chief diplomat warns ahead of Trump, Zelenskyy deal
item.description: The EU's chief diplomat has warned President Trump that Russian President Putin "doesn't really want peace" as Trump heads into a deal with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy expected to sign minerals deal Friday: Trump
item.description: Details are vague but what is known is that the United States is requesting revenue from Ukraine's rare minerals.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after a long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical condition Saturday after he suffered a prolonged asthmatic respiratory crisis while being treated for pneumonia and a complex lung infection, the Vatican said. The 88-year-old pope, who remains conscious, received “high flows” of oxygen to help him breathe.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, WGN 9
